title: The Myanmar Pearl Rules
text:
THE GOVERNMENT OF THE UNION OF MYANMAR
MINISTRY OF MINES
The Myanmar Pearl Rules
(15th June, 2000)

The Myanmar Pearl Rules
INDEX

Refusal Thereof

The Government of the Union of Myanmar
The Ministry of Mines
Notification No. 48/2000
Yangon. the 14th Waxing of Nayone, 1362 M.E
(15th June , 2000)
In exercise of the power conferred under sub-section(a), section 38 of the
Myanmar Pearl Law, the Government of the Union of Myanmar, the Ministry of
Mines makes the following Rules with the approval of the Government-
Chapter I
Title and Definition
1. These Rules shall be called the Myanmar Pearl Rules.
2. The expressions contained in these Rules shall have the same meanings as are
assigned to them in the Myanmar Pearl Law.
(a) “Law” means the Myanmar Pearl Law.
(b) “Holder of a Permit” means a person or an organization who/which has
obtained permission to carry out any or more of the operations for oyster
fishing and collecting, artificial breeding of oysters, oysters rearing, pearl
culturing, pearl harvesting or marketing of shell.
(c ) “Holder of a Registration Certificate” means a person who has obtained a
Registration Certificate issued under the law, for carrying out any
operation mentioned in section 9 of the Law.
(d) “Committee” means the Committee for Survey and Demarcation of the
Boundary of the Oyster Fishing Ground.
(e) “Form” means the form attached to these Rules.

2
Chapter II
Application for Permit, Granting and Refusal Thereof
3. (a) A person or an organization desirous of carrying out any or more of the
operations for oyster fishing and collecting, artificial breeding of oysters,
oyster rearing, pearl culturing, pearl harvesting or marketing of shell with
foreign investment under sub-section (a), section 4 of the Law shall apply
to the Ministry of Mines, after entering fully in the application form (Ma-
Pa-La-1) for obtaining a permit.
(b) A person or an organization desirous of carrying out any or more of the
operations for artificial breeding of oysters, oyster rearing, pearl culturing
or pearl harvesting with local investment under sub-section(b) section 4
of the Law shall apply to the Ministry of Mines after entering fully in the
application form (Ma-Pa-La-2) for obtaining a permit.
4. The Ministry of Mines shall-
(a) make necessary scrutiny of the application under Rule 3 and submit to the
Government with its remark.
(b) inform the application to execute an agreement for granting the permit in
accordance with the approval of the Government.
(c ) cause the applicant to execute an agreement with the Myanma Pearl
Enterprise.
(d) after executing an agreement under sub-rule (c ) and on receiving the
prescribed fee from the applicant, issue to the applicant a permit in Form
(Ma-Pa-La-3) for pearl production operation carried out with foreign
investment and a permit in Form (Ma-Pa-La-4) for pearl production
operation carried out with local investment.

3
5. (a) A person or an organization desirous of carrying out oyster fishing and
collecting operation with local investment shall apply to the Managing
Director, after entering fully in the application Form (Ma-Pa-La-5) for
obtaining a permit.
(b) A person or an organization desirous of carrying out marketing of shell
operation with local investment shall apply to the Managing Director,
after entering fully in the application Form (Ma-Pa-La-6) for obtaining a
permit.
6. The Managing Director-
(a) shall scrutinize as to whether or not the particulars contained in the
application are complete and correct.
(b) may, if necessary interrogate (examine)the relevant applicant in respect of
the particulars contained in the application.
(c ) may grant or refuse the permit.
(d) on receiving the prescribed fee from the applicant issue to the applicant a
permit in Form (Ma-Pa-La-7)for oyster fishing and collecting operation
carried out with local investment and a permit in Form (Ma-Pa-La-8) for
marketing of shell operation with local investment.
7. The tenure of the permit for carrying out the relevant pearl production operation
or marketing of shell operation with foreign investment or with local investment shall
be the same as the period permitted by the Ministry of Mines.
Chapter III
Duties of the Holder of a Permit
8. The holder of a permit-
(a) shall, within 90 days from the date of receiving the permit issued under
Rule 4 draw up a programme in respect of the operation contained in the
permit and submit to the Ministry of Mines.

4
(b) shall implement the operation contained in the permit in accordance with
the work (operation) programme submitted to the Ministry of Mines
under sub-rule(a).
(c ) if unable to implement within 90 days from the date of receiving the
permit in respect of the operation contained in the permit, has the right to
submit to the Ministry of Mines through the Managing Director for
extension of the date for commencement of implemention, stating the
reasons therefor.
(d) shall surrender the permit issued to the Ministry of Mines if unable to
implement the operation contained in the permit even though 90 days
from the date of receiving the permit issued under rule 4 has lapsed and
the Ministry of Mines has not allowed the extension of the date.
9. The holder of a permit-
(a) has the right to use in his pearl production only the quantity of oysters
permitted yearly (year-wise) in the bi-lateral agreement executed with the
Myanma Pearl Enterprise in respect of the artificial breeding of oysters.
The surplus spat shall be disposed of in accordance with the supervision
and guidance of the Myanma Pearl Enterprise.
(b) A technician recognized by the Myanma Pearl Enterprise or a person who
has obtained a registration certificate as a pearl culturing technician shall
be used in the pearl production.
10. The holder of a permit shall, within 30 days from the date of receiving the
permit pay the following fees to the Myanma Pearl Enterprise-
(a) the fee to be paid for a permit to carry out any or more of the operations
for oyster fishing and collecting, artificial breeding of oysters, oyster
rearing, pearl culturing or pearl harvesting, with foreign investment is
US$ 100000 (US dollars one hundred thousand only)and the Performance

5
Bank Guarantee for the operation is US$ 100000 (US dollars one
hundred thousand only);
(b) the fee to be paid for a permit to carry out any or more of the operations
for artificial breeding of oysters, oyster rearing, pearl culturing or pearl
harvesting with local investment is kyats 1000000 (kyats one million
only) and the Performance Bank Guarantee for the operation is kyats
1000000 (kyats one million only);
(c ) the fee to be paid for a permit to carry out oyster fishing with local
investment is kyats 20000 (kyats twenty thousand only);
(d) the fee to be paid for a permit to carry out marketing of shell with local
investment is kyats 100000 (kyats one hundred thousand only).
11. The Ministry of Mines may, with the approval of the Government and by
notification revise the fee and the deposit for a permit to carry out pearl production.
Chapter IV
Application for Registration Certificate and Granting Thereof
12. A person or an organization desirous of applying for permission to register for
carrying out any of the following operations shall enter fully in-
(a) application Form (Ma-Pa-La-9) for utilizing a vessel as oyster diving
vessel;
(b) application Form (Ma-Pa-La-10) for working as a pearl culturing
technician;
(c ) application Form (Ma-Pa-La-11) for working as a pearl culturing
(diving)technician and apply to the Managing Director.
13. The Managing Director-
(a) shall scrutinize as to whether or not the particulars in the application
under Rule 12 are complete and correct.

6
(b) may, if necessary interrogate (examine) the relevant applicant in respect
of the particulars contained in the application.
(c ) may grant or refuse to grant the registration certificate.
(d) shall, on receiving the registration fee and other fee from the applicant
issue to the applicant a registration certificate for oyster diving vessel in
Form (Ma-Pa-La-12), a registration certificate for pearl culturing
technician in Form (Ma-Pa-La-13)and a registration certificate for pearl
culturing (diving) technician in Form (Ma-Pa-La-14).
14. The registration fee according to the following types of operation is as shown
against each-
(a) one oyster diving vessel ---------------- kyat 3000
(b) one pearl culturing technician ---------------- kyat 500
(c ) one pearl culturing (diving) technician ---------------- kyat 500
15. The annual fee according to the following types of operation is as shown against
each:-
(a) one oyster diving vessel ---------------- kyat 2000
(b) one pearl culturing technician ---------------- kyat 500
(c ) one pearl culturing (diving) technician ---------------- kyat 500
16. The registration fee according to the following types of operation carried out
with foreign investment is as shown against each:-
(a) one oyster diving vessel ---------------- US$ 1000
(b) one pearl culturing technician ---------------- US$ 200
(c ) one pearl culturing (diving) technician ---------------- US$ 200

7
17. The annual fee according to the following types of operation carried out with
foreign investment is as shown against each:-
(a) one oyster diving vessel ---------------- US$ 500
(b) one pearl culturing technician ---------------- US$ 100
(c ) one pearl culturing (diving) technician ---------------- US$ 100
18. The Ministry of Mines may, with the approval of the Government and by
notification revise the registration fee and annual fee mentioned in Rule 14, Rule 15,
Rule 16 and Rule 17.
19. (a) The tenure of the registration certificate is the same as the period
mentioned in such registration certificate.
(b) An applicant for a registration certificate may apply for a maximum of 3
years as the tenure of the registration certificate according to his wish.
20. The holder of a registration certificate shall, if he wishes to continue the
operation on the expiry of the tenure of the registration certificate apply to the
Managing Director for extension of the tenure at least 30 days prior to the expiry of
the tenure.
21. The Managing Director-
(a) may, after scrutinizing the application for extension of the tenure of the
registration certificate permit or refuse the extension of the tenure.
(b) Shall, if the extension of the tenure of the registration certificate is
permitted cause payment of the prescribed annual fee to be made and
extend the tenure.
Chapter V
Duties of the Holder of a Registration Certificate
22. The holder of a registration certificate shall-
(a) abide by the terms and conditions of the registration certificate.

8
(b) pay in full the registration fee and annual fee prescribed by the Ministry
of Mines according to the type of the relevant operation on the day of
obtaining the registration certificate.
Chapter VI
Designation of the Oyster Fishing Ground
23. The Ministry of Mines shall-
(a) form the Committee for Survey and Demarcation of the Boundary of the
Oyster Fishing Ground consisting of the following persons, in order to
carry out the work of survey and demarcation of the boundary of the
oyster fishing ground-
(1) Managing Director ---------------- Chairman
Myanma Pearl Enterprise
(2) One representative ---------------- Member
from the Department of Mines,
Ministry of Mines
(3) One representative ---------------- Member
from the Department of Fisheries, Ministry of Livestock
Breeding and Fisheries
(4) One representative ---------------- Member
from the office of the Commander-in Chief (Navy) Ministry of
Defence
(5) One representative from ----------------- Member
the relevant Divisional Command
(6) One representative ---------------- Member
from the relevant State/Divisional Peace
and Development Council

9
(7) One to Three Experts in ---------------- Member
respect of pearl enterprise (operation)
(8) Deputy General Manager ---------------- Secretary
Myanma Pearl Enterprise
(b) It may re-constitute the Committee, if necessary.
24. The Committee shall compile a report for survey and demarcation of the
boundary of the Oyster Fishing Ground and submit the same to the Ministry of Mines.
25. The Ministry of Mines shall-
(a) co-ordinate with the Ministry of Livestock Breeding and Fisheries in
respect of the report for survey and demarcation of the boundary of the
oyster fishing ground and submit to the Government with its remarks.
(b) on obtaining the approval of the Government declare by notification the
survey and demarcation of the boundary of the oyster fishing ground.
26. If the Ministry of Mines is desirous of making survey and demarcation of the
boundary of the oyster fishing ground in the territorial waters which is administered by
any Government department or organization, it shall before doing so-
(a) make prior co-ordination with the relevant Government department or
organization and obtain the opinion thereof.
(b) insert as a condition in the permit, the particulars which the relevant
Government department or organization wishes to include (insert) under
the work requirement.
27. The Ministry of Mines-
(a) may modify (alter) or cancel the demarcation of the boundary of the
whole or part of the oyster fishing ground with the approval of the
Government . Such modification (alternation) or cancellation shall be
declared by notification.

10
(b) The map indicating the area of the oyster fishing ground which includes
the boundaries of the designated oyster fishing ground shall be distributed
to the relevant Government departments.
Chapter VII
Miscellaneous
28. In exercise of the powers conferred under section 15 of the Law, the Ministry of
Mines may, from time to time issue necessary prohibitions to protect and conserve
oyster fishing grounds from destruction and oyster from extinction.
29. The Ministry of Mines may, in respect of pearl production exempt vessels
conducting scientific research from payment of fees.
30. The Managing Director may, from time to time amend (alter) the terms and
conditions contained in the registration certificate.
31. The Myanma Pearl Enterprise may, with the approval of the Ministry of Mines
display and sell pearls within the country and abroad.
32. The Chief Inspector who is the Managing Director shall report to the Ministry
of Mines on his activities.
33. The Inspectors shall report to the Chief Inspector on their activities.
Sd/-
Brigadier-General Ohn Myint
Minister
The Government of the Union of Myanmar
Ministry of Mines

[ Rule 6 (d) ]
Permit No: --------------------- Dated ------------------------
The Government of the Union of Myanmar, Ministry of Mines hereby issues a
Permit to the following person/ organization to carry out Marketing of Shell with local
investment under section 7 of the Myanmar Pearl Law:-
1. Name, Designation, Responsibility, -----------------------
Citizenship Scrutiny Card Number, -----------------------
and Address of Person who has -----------------------
obtained a Permit -----------------------
2. Name and Registration Number of -----------------------
Company which has obtained -----------------------
a Permit and Address of Place -----------------------
where Company is situated -----------------------
3. Address of Factory, Warehouse (if any) -----------------------
of the Shell finished goods of the Person -----------------------
who has obtained a Permit -----------------------
4. Address of Sales shop (if any) of the -----------------------
Shell finished goods of the Person -----------------------
who has obtained a Permit -----------------------
5. Tenure Permitted -----------------------
6. Date of Permission -----------------------

7. Date of Expiry of Tenure -----------------------
8. Conditions contained in the Permit
(a) The Myanma Pearl Enterprise which has the right under the Law to
produce and sell lawfully and local and foreign Joint-Venture
Management Committees which have been assigned responsibility
by the Myanma Pearl Enterprise have the lawful right to sell shell
raw materials in accordance with the stipulations. There is the right
to re-sell such shells as finished goods or as shells. Natural oysters
shall not be finished, killed and shell extracted with-out permission.
Unlawful marketing of shells shall not be carried out.
(b) Local and Foreign Re-sale of shells shall be carried out in
accordance with existing laws.
(c ) Shall also abide by the conditions stipulated from time to time.
Signature -----------------------
Name -----------------------
Managing Director -----------------------

14. A person who has obtained a Registration Certificate shall comply with

issued by the Ministry of Mines and the Myanma Pearl Enterprise.
15. Conditions contained in the Registration Certificate (Shall also abide by
the statements attached and the stipulations from time to time).
Signature -----------------------
Name -----------------------
Managing Director

Conditions
(1) This Registration Certificate shall always be kept at the place of work
(Worksite).
(2) The Registration Certificate shall not be transferred, lent or sold to any
other person.
(3) On interrogation and scrutiny by responsible persons, the Registration
Certificate shall be produced. The list of names of the crew and the divers
who have been registered shall be submitted together with the cards
issued by the department and on which the photographs are affixed.
(4) No additions or alternations shall be made on the Registration Certificate.
(5) The Myanma Pearl Enterprise shall be informed immediately in case of
loss or destruction of the Registration Certificate.
(6) In order that the person who has obtained a Registration Certificate may
confirm the fishing ground in which he is to dive, he shall obtain prior
confirmation once a month from the relevant Branch office of the
Myanmar Pearl Enterprise. He shall carry out the oyster fishing operation
only after he has obtained confirmation.
(7) On obtaining naturally formed pearl, cultured pearl and shell he shall
deliver the same to the relevant Branch office of the Myanma Pearl
Enterprise, instead of keeping (holding) them in his possession.
(8) He shall not dive for oyster in any place other than in the permitted
fishing ground.
(9) He shall abide by the conditions stipulated from time to time.

shall submit to inspection, if necessary.
(3) If it is desired to continue the operation after expiry of the tenure of the
Registration Certificate, the holder shall apply again for a new
Registration Certificate at least (30)days prior to the expiry of the tenure.
(4) There is no right to carry out an operation with a Registration Certificate
that has exceeded its permitted tenure.
(5) There is no right to transfer and hold a Registration Certificate.
(6) Shall also abide by the conditions stipulated from time to time.
